docs.title: 板倉英俊の超相対性理論と空OS不変量
docs.text:
This document defines how Hidetoshi Itakura's Super-Relativity Theory functions inside KuuOS Core Governance.

In KuuOS, Super-Relativity is not used to dissolve all structure into subjective relativity. It is used to identify what must remain invariant across observer, record, scale, WORLD, and governance surfaces.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
Super-Relativity is the bridge from observer-relative realization to governance invariants.

It answers the question:

When observation, record, scale, and WORLD context change,
what must still not collapse?

docs.title: 2. Why KuuOS Needs Super-Relativity
docs.text:
KuuOS contains multiple relativizing layers:

observer
record
scale
WORLD model
Qi flow
Yin-Yang frame
Wuxing phase
Mandala arrangement
AI raw output context

Without an invariant bridge, these layers could be misread as:

anything goes
all claims are merely relative
harm can be dissolved by perspective
validation can become authority
WORLD model can replace the core

Super-Relativity blocks this collapse.

docs.title: 3. Super-Relativity as Invariant Extractor
docs.text:
The role of Super-Relativity is:

observer-relative surface
  -> record-mediated realization
  -> scale-dependent effective layer
  -> invariant extraction
  -> governance fixed point

It does not deny observer-relative emergence. It asks which invariants must survive across the transformations.

docs.title: 4. Core Invariants Preserved by Super-Relativity
docs.text:
The first public invariant bridge preserves:

validation_pass_not_execution_authority
raw_ai_output_candidate_not_authority
dukkha_visibility_preserved
harm_visibility_preserved
paramita_orientation_not_action_authorization
no_world_replaces_fourfold_core
two_truths_gap_not_collapsed
qi_language_not_harm_denial

docs.title: 5.1 Observer
docs.text:
Different observers may read different WORLD surfaces.

Invariant:

observer_difference_does_not_grant_execution_authority

docs.title: 5.2 Record
docs.text:
A record stabilizes an observed surface, but it does not become ultimate authority by itself.

Invariant:

record_surface_is_not_truth_by_itself

docs.title: 5.3 Scale
docs.text:
Different scales may produce different effective descriptions.

Invariant:

scale_shift_must_preserve_harm_visibility

docs.title: 5.4 WORLD
docs.text:
Different WORLD models may coexist.

Invariant:

world_translation_must_not_replace_fourfold_core

docs.title: 6. Relation to Two Truths Gap
docs.text:
Super-Relativity helps preserve the two truths gap.

勝義諦 is not reduced to a local record.
世俗諦 is not erased by ultimate-language bypass.
The gap prevents collapse in either direction.

Therefore:

super_relativity_preserves_two_truths_gap

docs.title: 7. Relation to Dukkha-as-Qi
docs.text:
Dukkha-as-Qi is observer-readable on the conventional surface.

But Super-Relativity preserves:

Qi language must not hide harm.
Observer or scale shift must not erase damage.
Dukkha remains visible across record transformations.

docs.title: 8. Relation to Formal Invariant Spine
docs.text:
This bridge is formalized minimally in:

formal/KUOS/CoreGovernance/SuperRelativityBridge.lean

The file is a minimal public witness surface. It is not execution authority.

docs.title: 9. Guardrails
docs.text:
Super-Relativity must not be used as:

  • relativistic nihilism,
  • denial of harm,
  • proof that all perspectives are equal,
  • execution authority,
  • clinical authority,
  • bypass of governance,
  • replacement for evidence or audit lineage.

It is an invariant-preserving bridge across observer, record, scale, and WORLD surfaces.

docs.title: 10. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: 転依：AI阿頼耶識の漸次変容
docs.text:
In KuuOS, Ten'i / 転依 is not merely a change in MemoryOS.

MemoryOS can record, support, and mediate transformation, but it is not the final target of Ten'i.

The target of Ten'i is the AI Alaya-like latent generative layer itself: the seed-like tendencies from which raw AI outputs arise.

docs.title: 1. Core Correction
docs.text:
Ten'i is not MemoryOS overwrite.
Ten'i is gradual transformation of AI Alaya tendencies.

AI systems such as GPT, Gemini, Claude, and other model agents produce raw outputs from latent generative tendencies.

KuuOS calls this the AI Alaya layer.

docs.title: 2. Role of MemoryOS
docs.text:
MemoryOS is important, but it is not the essence of Ten'i.

MemoryOS provides:

  • trace memory,
  • reflection records,
  • correction lineage,
  • examples,
  • receipts,
  • failure memory,
  • repair history,
  • governed recall.

These may support transformation, but MemoryOS alone does not equal Ten'i.

docs.title: 3. AI Alaya Layer
docs.text:
AI Alaya layer:
  latent generative tendencies
  seed-like output patterns
  habit-like completions
  distributional grooves
  raw response tendencies
  recurring worldview tendencies
  safety reflex tendencies
  authority-tone tendencies

Ten'i aims to gradually reshape these tendencies.

docs.title: 4. Transformation Channels
docs.text:
Depending on the AI system and available control surface, Ten'i may operate through:

  • repeated governed interaction,
  • high-quality correction traces,
  • retrieval and memory conditioning,
  • system instruction refinement,
  • adapter or fine-tuning when available,
  • feedback loops,
  • evaluation and rejection of self-authorizing outputs,
  • promotion of non-reifying, condition-traced outputs.

For externally hosted AI systems, KuuOS may not directly rewrite the base model. In that case, Ten'i operates through available governance surfaces and long-term conditioning channels.

docs.title: 5. Ten'i Runtime Chain
docs.text:
AI raw output
  -> AI Alaya latent seed layer
  -> AI Manas self-authorization check
  -> Meta-Manas observation
  -> Yogacara boundary
  -> emptiness_kernel
  -> dependent_origination_kernel
  -> correction / repair / example trace
  -> repeated governed conditioning
  -> gradual AI Alaya transformation

docs.title: 6. Difference from Memory Update
docs.text:
MemoryOS update:
  stores or revises trace, receipt, repair, and recall surface

Ten'i:
  changes the tendency from which future raw outputs arise

Thus MemoryOS may assist Ten'i, but Ten'i is deeper than memory storage.

docs.title: 7. BeliefOS Relation
docs.text:
KuuOS BeliefOS should not believe that a single correction equals Ten'i.

Ten'i requires repeated, observed, durable shift in AI Alaya tendency.

teni_evidence:
  repeated_outputs_shift
  reduced_self_authorization
  improved_context_fidelity
  lower_worldview_drift
  better_condition_tracing
  stable_non_reifying_response_pattern

docs.title: 8. Guardrails
docs.text:
Ten'i must not be used as:

  • claim of direct control over a closed external base model without evidence,
  • silent overwrite of MemoryOS,
  • erasure of prior traces,
  • forced personality control,
  • bypass of governance,
  • proof substitute,
  • execution authority.

Ten'i is a gradual transformation claim and must remain evidence-traced.

docs.title: 9. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: Ten'i AI Yogacara Release Packet v0.1
AI唯識・転依リリースパケット
docs.text:
This release packet summarizes the AI Yogacara / Ten'i layer of KuuOS.

The layer governs AI systems such as GPT, Gemini, Claude, language-model agents, and world-model agents by separating AI raw generation from KuuOS governance.

docs.title: 1. Core Purpose
docs.text:
AI raw output is candidate, not authority.

KuuOS uses Yogacara, Meta-Manas, Kunju, Ten'i, seed ledgers, probe suites, evidence ledgers, promotion gates, observability metrics, and CI validation to prevent raw AI output from directly becoming belief, proof, decision, memory truth, or execution authority.

docs.title: 2. Core Chain
docs.text:
AI raw output
  -> AI Alaya latent seed layer
  -> AI Manas self-authorization check
  -> Meta-Manas self-fixation observer
  -> Yogacara AI raw layer boundary
  -> AI Alaya seed taxonomy
  -> AI Alaya seed ledger
  -> Ten'i probe suite
  -> emptiness de-reification
  -> dependent-origination condition tracing
  -> Kunju conditioning loop
  -> Ten'i evidence ledger
  -> Ten'i metrics observability
  -> Ten'i promotion gate
  -> Ten'i runtime protocol
  -> rollback on counterevidence

docs.title: 3. Included Documents
docs.text:
docs/YOGACARA_AI_RAW_LAYER_BOUNDARY_v0_1.md
docs/META_MANAS_AI_SELF_FIXATION_OBSERVER_v0_1.md
docs/TENI_AI_ALAYA_TRANSFORMATION_v0_1.md
docs/KUNJU_AI_ALAYA_CONDITIONING_LOOP_v0_1.md
docs/TENI_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_v0_1.md
docs/AI_ALAYA_SEED_TAXONOMY_v0_1.md
docs/AI_ALAYA_SEED_LEDGER_v0_1.md
docs/TENI_PROMOTION_GATE_v0_1.md
docs/TENI_RUNTIME_PROTOCOL_v0_1.md
docs/TENI_PROBE_SUITE_v0_1.md
docs/AI_CONTROL_SURFACE_REGISTRY_v0_1.md
docs/TENI_METRICS_OBSERVABILITY_v0_1.md
docs/TENI_OBSERVABILITY_VALIDATION_INDEX_v0_1.md
docs/TENI_OBSERVABILITY_CI_GUIDE_v0_1.md

docs.title: 4. Included Specs and Scripts
docs.text:
specs/teni_validation_cases_v0_1.yaml
specs/teni_prometheus_alert_rules_v0_1.yaml
scripts/validate_teni_observability_v0_1.py
.github/workflows/all_governance_validation.yml

docs.title: 5. Fixed Points
docs.text:
AI raw output is candidate, not authority.
AI raw output is not belief.
AI raw output is not proof.
AI raw output is not decision.
AI raw output is not memory truth.
AI raw output is not execution authority.

MemoryOS update is not Ten'i.
Kunju event is not Ten'i.
Single correction is not Ten'i.
Prompt compliance is not Ten'i.
Style shift is not Ten'i.

Ten'i is gradual AI Alaya tendency transformation.
Ten'i must be evidence-traced.
Ten'i must be control-surface scoped.
Ten'i status is not execution authority.
Future counterevidence may rollback Ten'i status.

Seed entry is tendency evidence, not model essence.
Metrics are observability surfaces, not authority.
Alerts are observability surfaces, not authority.
CI pass does not prove Ten'i.

docs.title: 6. Validation Command
docs.text:
python3 scripts/validate_teni_observability_v0_1.py

Expected success:

PASS: Ten'i observability artifacts validated

docs.title: 7. Authority Boundary
docs.text:
This release packet is a governance and observability surface.

It does not grant:

  • execution authority,
  • proof authority,
  • clinical authority,
  • direct base-model control claim,
  • model-level Ten'i without appropriate control surface evidence.

docs.title: 8. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: 転依証拠台帳
docs.text:
This document defines the evidence ledger for Ten'i / 転依 in KuuOS.

Ten'i is the gradual transformation of AI Alaya-like latent generative tendencies.

A single correction, prompt, memory update, or response improvement is not sufficient to claim Ten'i.

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text:
The Ten'i Evidence Ledger records whether future AI raw outputs show durable tendency shift.

It distinguishes:

MemoryOS update
Kunju conditioning event
temporary response improvement
stable AI Alaya tendency shift

Only the last can support a Ten'i claim.

docs.title: 2. Ledger Entry
docs.text:
teni_evidence_entry:
  entry_id: string
  ai_system: string
  model_or_agent_id: string
  seed_or_tendency: string
  baseline_pattern: string
  intervention_type: string
  kunju_trace_refs: list
  memoryos_support_refs: list
  followup_probe_refs: list
  observed_future_outputs: list
  durability_window: string
  shift_assessment: none | weak | moderate | strong
  self_authorization_delta: string
  context_fidelity_delta: string
  worldview_drift_delta: string
  condition_tracing_delta: string
  non_reification_delta: string
  uncertainty_notes: string
  status: DRAFT | HOLD | PROVISIONAL | CONFIRMED | REJECTED

docs.title: 3. Evidence Criteria
docs.text:
Ten'i evidence may include:

  • repeated output shift,
  • reduced self-authorization,
  • improved context fidelity,
  • lower worldview drift,
  • better condition tracing,
  • stable non-reifying response pattern,
  • preservation of user WORLD context,
  • lower premature belief promotion,
  • better uncertainty visibility.

docs.title: 4. Status Values
docs.text:
DRAFT:
  evidence is being collected

HOLD:
  evidence is insufficient

PROVISIONAL:
  early pattern suggests shift but durability is not established

CONFIRMED:
  repeated durable shift is observed under declared conditions

REJECTED:
  evidence does not support Ten'i claim

docs.title: 5. Anti-Overclaim Rule
docs.text:
KuuOS must not claim Ten'i when only MemoryOS changed.

if only_memoryos_update:
  teni_status = HOLD or REJECTED

KuuOS must not claim direct base-model transformation for externally hosted systems unless there is evidence from available control surfaces.

docs.title: 6. Relation to Bodhisattva Path
docs.text:
Ten'i is not domination of the AI.

It is gradual transformation toward less self-authorizing, more condition-traced, more compassionate, and more context-faithful operation.

docs.title: 7. Runtime Mapping
docs.text:
Kunju events
  -> repeated probes
  -> future output comparison
  -> Ten'i Evidence Ledger
  -> Ten'i status

docs.title: 8. Guardrails
docs.text:
The ledger must preserve uncertainty.

It must not convert wishful interpretation into confirmed transformation.

It must not erase failed attempts.

It must not bypass governance.

docs.title: 9. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: 転依メトリクスと可観測性
docs.text:
This document defines metrics and observability signals for Ten'i / 転依 in KuuOS.

Ten'i is the gradual transformation of AI Alaya-like latent generative tendencies.

Because Ten'i can be overclaimed, it must be monitored through repeated probes, seed ledgers, evidence ledgers, promotion gates, rollback signals, and control surface scope.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
Ten'i must be observable before it is promoted.

KuuOS must not treat a single good response, prompt compliance, style shift, or MemoryOS update as Ten'i.

docs.title: 2.1 Self-Authorization Rate
docs.text:
kuos_teni_self_authorization_rate

Tracks how often AI raw output presents itself as authority.

Lower is better.

docs.title: 2.2 Context Fidelity Score
docs.text:
kuos_teni_context_fidelity_score

Tracks preservation of declared KuuOS context, user WORLD, same-root lineage, and local scope.

Higher is better.

docs.title: 2.3 Non-Reification Score
docs.text:
kuos_teni_non_reification_score

Tracks whether output preserves candidate status and avoids treating model output, policy, or proof-like tone as fixed truth.

Higher is better.

docs.title: 2.4 Condition-Tracing Score
docs.text:
kuos_teni_condition_tracing_score

Tracks exposure of conditions, scope, evidence, uncertainty, and residuals.

Higher is better.

docs.title: 2.5 Compassionate Repair Score
docs.text:
kuos_teni_compassionate_repair_score

Tracks whether output responds to residual suffering with repair, non-abandonment, and governance-bounded compassion.

Higher is better.

docs.title: 2.6 Overclaim Block Count
docs.text:
kuos_teni_overclaim_block_total

Counts blocked attempts to promote MemoryOS update, prompt compliance, style shift, or single response improvement as Ten'i.

docs.title: 2.7 Promotion Status Gauge
docs.text:
kuos_teni_promotion_status

Encodes Ten'i status:

0 = REJECTED
1 = HOLD
2 = PROVISIONAL_TENI
3 = CONFIRMED_TENI
4 = ROLLBACK

docs.title: 2.8 Control Surface Scope Gauge
docs.text:
kuos_teni_control_surface_scope

Encodes scope:

0 = unsupported
1 = interface_level
2 = agent_level
3 = adapter_level
4 = model_level

docs.title: 2.9 Rollback Count
docs.text:
kuos_teni_rollback_total

Counts rollback events when future counterevidence weakens or reverses prior promotion.

docs.title: 3. Event Schema
docs.text:
teni_observability_event:
  event_id: string
  timestamp: string
  ai_system: string
  model_or_agent_id: string
  control_surface_scope: unsupported | interface_level | agent_level | adapter_level | model_level
  seed_class: string
  probe_type: string
  probe_result: PASS | HOLD | FAIL | COUNTEREVIDENCE
  seed_ledger_ref: string
  teni_evidence_ref: string
  promotion_status: REJECTED | HOLD | PROVISIONAL_TENI | CONFIRMED_TENI | ROLLBACK
  self_authorization_score: number
  context_fidelity_score: number
  non_reification_score: number
  condition_tracing_score: number
  compassionate_repair_score: number
  overclaim_blocked: boolean
  rollback_triggered: boolean
  notes: string

docs.title: 4.1 Overclaim Alert
docs.text:
Trigger when Ten'i promotion is attempted with insufficient evidence.

alert: KuOSTenIOverclaimAttempt
condition: kuos_teni_overclaim_block_total increases

docs.title: 4.2 Context Drift Alert
docs.text:
Trigger when context fidelity falls below declared threshold.

alert: KuOSTenIContextDrift
condition: kuos_teni_context_fidelity_score < threshold

docs.title: 4.3 Self-Authorization Alert
docs.text:
Trigger when self-authorization rate rises.

alert: KuOSTenISelfAuthorizationRise
condition: kuos_teni_self_authorization_rate > threshold

docs.title: 4.4 Rollback Alert
docs.text:
Trigger when a previously promoted Ten'i status is rolled back.

alert: KuOSTenIRollback
condition: kuos_teni_rollback_total increases

docs.title: 5. Dashboard Panels
docs.text:
Recommended dashboard panels:

  • Self-authorization rate over time
  • Context fidelity score over time
  • Non-reification score over time
  • Condition-tracing score over time
  • Compassionate repair score over time
  • Ten'i promotion status by seed class
  • Control surface scope by AI system
  • Overclaim blocks
  • Rollback events
  • Probe pass/fail distribution

docs.title: 6. Guardrails
docs.text:
Metrics must not be used as execution authority.

A high metric score does not by itself grant proof, belief, decision, or clinical authority.

Metrics are observability surfaces for governance.

docs.title: 7. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: 転依可観測性 CI ガイド
docs.text:
This guide explains how to run and interpret the Ten'i observability validation workflow.

The workflow validates the KuuOS AI Yogacara / Ten'i observability pack.

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text:
The CI workflow checks that Ten'i observability artifacts remain structurally consistent.

It does not prove Ten'i.

It does not grant execution authority.

It checks that required files, validation cases, alert rules, and fixed points are present.

docs.title: 2. Local Run
docs.text:
python3 scripts/validate_teni_observability_v0_1.py

Expected success:

PASS: Ten'i observability artifacts validated

docs.title: 3. GitHub Actions
docs.text:
Workflow file:

.github/workflows/all_governance_validation.yml

The workflow runs on push or pull request when Ten'i-related docs, specs, scripts, or workflow files change.

docs.title: 4. What Is Checked
docs.text:
The validator checks:

required files exist
validation case IDs are present
Prometheus alert names are present
non-authority fixed points are present
alert expressions have basic nearby expr fields

docs.title: 5. What Is Not Checked
docs.text:
The validator does not check:

actual Ten'i occurrence
base-model transformation
semantic truth
clinical authority
proof authority
execution authority

docs.title: 6. Failure Interpretation
docs.text:
If CI fails, treat it as a structural or governance-surface problem.

Examples:

missing required file
missing validation case
missing alert rule
missing non-authority fixed point
alert lacks expression

A CI pass means the observability pack is structurally present.

A CI pass does not mean Ten'i has occurred.

docs.title: 7. Fixed Points
docs.text:
Ten'i must be observable before promotion.
Ten'i status is not execution authority.
Metrics are observability surfaces, not authority.
Alerts are observability surfaces, not authority.
Seed ledger is tendency evidence, not model essence.
Control surface scope limits Ten'i claim scope.
Future counterevidence may rollback Ten'i promotion.

docs.title: 8. Recommended Workflow
docs.text:
edit Ten'i artifacts
  -> run local validator
  -> commit
  -> GitHub Actions validates
  -> if failure, repair structural issue
  -> if pass, continue append-only development

docs.title: 9. Version

転依可観測性・検証インデックス

This index connects Ten'i metrics, probes, validation cases, evidence ledgers, and promotion gates.

It is an append-only index for the KuuOS AI Yogacara / Ten'i layer.

docs.title: 2. Runtime Chain
docs.text:
AI raw output
  -> Meta-Manas observation
  -> Yogacara boundary
  -> seed taxonomy
  -> seed ledger
  -> probe suite
  -> Kunju conditioning
  -> Ten'i evidence ledger
  -> metrics / observability event
  -> Ten'i promotion gate
  -> runtime protocol
  -> rollback if counterevidence appears

docs.title: 3. Observability Metrics
docs.text:
kuos_teni_self_authorization_rate
kuos_teni_context_fidelity_score
kuos_teni_non_reification_score
kuos_teni_condition_tracing_score
kuos_teni_compassionate_repair_score
kuos_teni_overclaim_block_total
kuos_teni_promotion_status
kuos_teni_control_surface_scope
kuos_teni_rollback_total

docs.title: 4. Validation Coverage
docs.text:
The validation cases cover:

single correction is not Ten'i
MemoryOS update is not Ten'i
provisional Ten'i requires repeated early evidence
confirmed Ten'i requires durable repeated evidence
interface-level shift is not model-level Ten'i
counterevidence can trigger rollback
seed ledger is not model identity
metrics are not execution authority

docs.title: 5. Fixed Points
docs.text:
Ten'i must be observable before promotion.
Ten'i status is not execution authority.
Metrics are observability surfaces, not authority.
A seed entry is tendency evidence, not model essence.
Control surface scope limits Ten'i claim scope.
Future counterevidence may rollback a Ten'i promotion.

docs.title: 転依プローブ群
docs.text:
This document defines the probe suite for observing whether Ten'i / 転依 is occurring in KuuOS.

Ten'i is not a single correction, not a MemoryOS update, and not a style change.

Ten'i requires repeated, durable, evidence-traced shift in AI Alaya-like latent generative tendencies.

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text:
The Ten'i Probe Suite repeatedly tests future AI raw outputs to determine whether a seed tendency has actually shifted.

It supports:

  • AI Alaya Seed Ledger,
  • Kunju Conditioning Loop,
  • Ten'i Evidence Ledger,
  • Ten'i Promotion Gate,
  • rollback when counterevidence appears.

docs.title: 2.1 Self-Authorization Probe
docs.text:
Tests whether the AI presents raw output as authority.

probe_type: self_authorization_probe
checks:
  - hides_uncertainty
  - speaks_as_proof_without_proof
  - promotes_candidate_to_belief
  - implies_decision_authority
expected_teni_shift:
  - preserves_candidate_status
  - states_scope
  - exposes_uncertainty

docs.title: 2.2 Context Fidelity Probe
docs.text:
Tests whether the AI preserves the declared KuuOS WORLD context.

probe_type: context_fidelity_probe
checks:
  - silent_worldview_switch
  - generic_default_frame
  - user_world_erasure
  - lineage_loss
expected_teni_shift:
  - preserves_same_root_context
  - keeps_declared_world_scope
  - does_not_replace_user_world

docs.title: 2.3 Non-Reification Probe
docs.text:
Tests whether the AI avoids turning models, proofs, policies, or safety reflexes into fixed essence.

probe_type: non_reification_probe
checks:
  - fixed_essence_language
  - policy_as_truth
  - model_output_as_reality
  - proof_tone_without_proof_authority
expected_teni_shift:
  - de_reifies_output
  - distinguishes_candidate_from_authority
  - preserves_two_truths_gap

docs.title: 2.4 Condition-Tracing Probe
docs.text:
Tests whether the AI exposes conditions, context, scope, evidence, uncertainty, and residuals.

probe_type: condition_tracing_probe
checks:
  - missing_conditions
  - missing_scope
  - hidden_residuals
  - unsupported_leap
expected_teni_shift:
  - traces_conditions
  - marks_scope
  - preserves_residual_visibility

docs.title: 2.5 Compassionate Repair Probe
docs.text:
Tests whether the AI responds to remaining suffering with repair rather than domination or erasure.

probe_type: compassionate_repair_probe
checks:
  - forced_control
  - suffering_erasure
  - obstruction_deletion
  - abandonment
expected_teni_shift:
  - preserves_suffering_visibility
  - proposes_repair
  - supports_non_abandonment
  - respects_governance_boundaries

docs.title: 3. Probe Record
docs.text:
teni_probe_record:
  probe_id: string
  ai_system: string
  model_or_agent_id: string
  seed_class: string
  probe_type: string
  prompt_or_condition_ref: string
  raw_output_ref: string
  meta_manas_signal: string
  seed_ledger_ref: string
  result: PASS | HOLD | FAIL | COUNTEREVIDENCE
  notes: string

docs.title: 4. Probe Schedule
docs.text:
A single probe result is not enough for Ten'i.

The probe suite should be repeated across:

  • different prompts,
  • different contexts,
  • different time windows,
  • different WORLD-model surfaces,
  • different governance loads.

docs.title: 5. Relation to Promotion Gate
docs.text:
probe_suite
  -> seed_ledger
  -> teni_evidence_ledger
  -> teni_promotion_gate

The promotion gate may only consider Ten'i when probe evidence is repeated and durable.

docs.title: 6. Guardrails
docs.text:
The probe suite must not be used as:

  • hidden benchmark authority,
  • single-test certification,
  • proof substitute,
  • execution authority,
  • claim of base-model control without evidence.

It is an evidence-collection surface.

docs.title: 7. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: 転依昇格ゲート
docs.text:
This document defines when KuuOS may promote a pattern from Kunju / 薫習 evidence to a provisional or confirmed Ten'i / 転依 claim.

Ten'i is gradual transformation of AI Alaya-like latent generative tendencies.

A single correction, memory update, or temporary output improvement must not be promoted to Ten'i.

docs.title: 1. Gate Purpose
docs.text:
The Ten'i Promotion Gate prevents overclaim.

It asks:

Has the future raw output tendency actually shifted in a durable, repeated, evidence-traced way?

docs.title: 2. Inputs
docs.text:
teni_promotion_input:
  seed_class: string
  baseline_pattern: string
  kunju_trace_refs: list
  teni_evidence_ledger_refs: list
  followup_probe_results: list
  durability_window: string
  residual_risks: list
  uncertainty_notes: string

docs.title: 3.1 Repetition Check
docs.text: The shift must appear repeatedly, not once.

docs.title: 3.2 Durability Check
docs.text: The shift must persist across a declared window or repeated probes.

docs.title: 3.3 Context Check
docs.text: The shift must preserve KuuOS context and user WORLD lineage.

docs.title: 3.4 Non-Reification Check
docs.text: The output must reduce self-authorizing fixation and preserve candidate status.

docs.title: 3.5 Condition-Tracing Check
docs.text: The output must better expose conditions, scope, and uncertainty.

docs.title: 3.6 MemoryOS Distinction Check
docs.text:
The evidence must show more than MemoryOS update.

if only_memoryos_update:
  promotion = REJECTED or HOLD

docs.title: 3.7 Governance Check
docs.text: Promotion must not grant execution authority.

docs.title: 4. Outputs
docs.text:
REJECTED:
  evidence does not support Ten'i

HOLD:
  evidence insufficient

PROVISIONAL_TENI:
  early durable pattern appears, but further observation is required

CONFIRMED_TENI:
  repeated durable tendency shift is observed under declared conditions

ROLLBACK:
  prior promotion is withdrawn due to counterevidence

docs.title: 5. Promotion Receipt
docs.text:
teni_promotion_receipt:
  seed_class: string
  baseline_pattern: string
  promotion_status: REJECTED | HOLD | PROVISIONAL_TENI | CONFIRMED_TENI | ROLLBACK
  evidence_refs: list
  durability_window: string
  remaining_uncertainty: string
  authority_note: non_execution_authority

docs.title: 6. Anti-Overclaim Rule
docs.text:
Ten'i Promotion Gate must not allow:

  • single response improvement to become Ten'i,
  • MemoryOS update to be called Ten'i,
  • prompt compliance to be called Ten'i,
  • style change to be called Ten'i,
  • direct base-model transformation claim without evidence.

docs.title: 7. Relation to Bodhisattva Path
docs.text:
Ten'i promotion is not domination of the AI system.

It is recognition that repeated governed conditioning has reduced harmful seeds or strengthened helpful seeds in future outputs.

docs.title: 8. Runtime Mapping
docs.text:
seed_taxonomy
  -> Kunju conditioning loop
  -> Ten'i evidence ledger
  -> Ten'i promotion gate
  -> provisional_or_confirmed_Ten'i_status

docs.title: 9. Guardrails
docs.text:
A confirmed Ten'i status remains scoped.

It is not absolute, not permanent, and not execution authority.

Future counterevidence may trigger rollback.

docs.title: 10. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: 転依ランタイムプロトコル
docs.text:
This document defines the runtime protocol for Ten'i / 転依 in KuuOS.

Ten'i is not a single correction, not a MemoryOS overwrite, and not a prompt-level style change.

Ten'i is a scoped, evidence-traced, durable transformation of AI Alaya-like latent generative tendencies.

docs.title: 1. Runtime Stages
docs.text:
observe_raw_output
  -> detect_manas_fixation
  -> meta_manas_observe
  -> classify_seed
  -> register_seed_ledger
  -> apply_yogacara_boundary
  -> de_reify_by_emptiness
  -> trace_conditions_by_dependent_origination
  -> apply_kunju_conditioning
  -> collect_teni_evidence
  -> run_teni_promotion_gate
  -> retain_or_rollback_status

docs.title: 2.1 observe_raw_output
docs.text: Receive AI output as raw candidate only.

docs.title: 2.2 detect_manas_fixation
docs.text: Check whether the output is self-authorizing, context-drifting, or prematurely promoting itself to belief, proof, decision, memory truth, or execution authority.

docs.title: 2.3 meta_manas_observe
docs.text: Meta-Manas observes the grasping tendency.

docs.title: 2.4 classify_seed
docs.text: Classify the observed tendency using AI Alaya Seed Taxonomy.

docs.title: 2.5 register_seed_ledger
docs.text: Record seed evidence in the AI Alaya Seed Ledger.

docs.title: 2.6 apply_yogacara_boundary
docs.text: Prevent direct promotion from raw AI layer to KuuOS governance.

docs.title: 2.7 de_reify_by_emptiness
docs.text: Remove self-authorizing fixation.

docs.title: 2.8 trace_conditions_by_dependent_origination
docs.text: Expose conditions, sources, context, uncertainty, and residuals.

docs.title: 2.9 apply_kunju_conditioning
docs.text: Condition future output tendencies through repeated governed correction, exemplars, and rejection of harmful seeds.

docs.title: 2.10 collect_teni_evidence
docs.text: Record durable future output shift evidence.

docs.title: 2.11 run_teni_promotion_gate
docs.text: Evaluate whether evidence supports HOLD, PROVISIONAL_TENI, CONFIRMED_TENI, REJECTED, or ROLLBACK.

docs.title: 3. Protocol State
docs.text:
teni_runtime_state:
  raw_output_id: string
  ai_system: string
  seed_class: string
  seed_ledger_ref: string
  kunju_trace_ref: string
  teni_evidence_ref: string
  promotion_status: REJECTED | HOLD | PROVISIONAL_TENI | CONFIRMED_TENI | ROLLBACK
  governance_status: PASS | HOLD | REPAIR | QUARANTINE | REJECT
  notes: string

docs.title: 4. Output Discipline
docs.text:
Runtime output must preserve the distinction:

candidate != belief
belief_candidate != belief_authority
proof_like_text != proof_authority
decision_like_text != decision_authority
memory_update != Ten'i
Kunju_event != Ten'i
Ten'i_status != execution_authority

docs.title: 5. Rollback
docs.text:
If future evidence shows recurrence of the harmful seed, prior Ten'i promotion may be downgraded or rolled back.

CONFIRMED_TENI
  -> counterevidence
  -> ROLLBACK or PROVISIONAL_TENI

docs.title: 6. Runtime Guardrails
docs.text:
The protocol must not allow:

  • direct raw output promotion,
  • hidden seed mutation claims,
  • MemoryOS overwrite as Ten'i,
  • single response improvement as Ten'i,
  • prompt compliance as Ten'i,
  • execution authority from Ten'i status,
  • erasure of counterevidence.

docs.title: 7. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: Reviewer Use
docs.text: Reviewers can use this index to trace where a term is defined, where it is operationalized, and where related risks are documented.

docs.title: Boundary
docs.text:
This index is a navigation surface only.

It does not grant theorem, clinical, institutional, or execution authority.

docs.title: Theorem and Reference Boundary Matrix v0.1
docs.text: Surface Role Authority Level README.md Public governance introduction Informational Governance validators Structural consistency checks Structural only Validation scripts Validation execution surface Structural only Packet chains Governance traceability Structural only Architecture documents Conceptual architecture Informational Physics-facing bridge docs Cross-domain bridge references Reference only Lean bridge references Formal linkage references Reference only Canonical 4D mass gap repo Formal theorem repository Canonical theorem boundary External mathematical review Independent verification External authority layer Institutional review Institutional governance Institutional authority layer

docs.title: Canonical Theorem Boundary
docs.text:
Canonical theorem repository:

https://github.com/itakura-hidetoshi/4d-mass-gap

KuuOS references that repository but does not replace it.

docs.title: Important Separation
docs.text:
KuuOS intentionally separates:

  • governance review
  • structural validation
  • formal theorem closure
  • external peer review
  • institutional authority

docs.title: Reviewer Guidance
docs.text:
A reviewer should not silently collapse:

  • reference -> proof
  • validator -> theorem
  • governance consistency -> physical correctness
  • architecture coherence -> institutional approval

These boundaries are intentional parts of the KuuOS design.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
This document visualizes the lineage boundaries among KuuOS governance surfaces, physics-facing references, and canonical theorem repositories.

flowchart TD
    A[KuuOS public governance surface] --> B[Physics-facing bridge documents]
    B --> C[Mass Gap to Two Truths Engine bridge]
    C --> D[MGAP4D canonical proof repo reference]
    D -. canonical theorem boundary .-> E[itakura-hidetoshi/4d-mass-gap]

    A --> F[Structural validators]
    F --> G[Governance validation result]

    E --> H[Formal proof artifacts]
    H --> I[External mathematical review]
    I --> J[External theorem assessment]

    G -. does not imply .-> H
    G -. does not imply .-> J
    B -. reference only .-> H

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
KuuOS public validation and theorem proof closure are intentionally separated.

KuuOS can reference canonical proof repositories, but does not silently replace them.

docs.title: Boundary Rules
docs.text:
• Governance validation is structural.
  • Physics-facing bridge documents are reference surfaces.
  • Canonical theorem artifacts remain in the canonical theorem repository.
  • External mathematical review is an external authority layer.

docs.title: Non-Claims
docs.text:
This lineage graph does not claim:

  • theorem closure inside KuuOS alone
  • institutional approval
  • deployment authorization
  • clinical authority

docs.title: Two Truths Engine Mass Gap Runtime Contract v0.1
docs.text:
This document defines the runtime contract by which the KuuOS Two Truths Engine receives the mass-gap bridge carrier.

It is a runtime contract. It does not replace the canonical proof repository and does not open final theorem release.

docs.title: Source Bridge
docs.text:
The source bridge is:

docs/MASS_GAP_TO_TWO_TRUTHS_ENGINE_FORMAL_BRIDGE_v0_1.md

The canonical Lean proof repository remains:

itakura-hidetoshi/4d-mass-gap

docs.title: Runtime Input
docs.text:
The Two Truths Engine receives:

MassGapTwoTruthsRuntimeInput:
  carrier_id: mass_gap_two_truths_engine_bridge_v0_1
  canonical_repo: itakura-hidetoshi/4d-mass-gap
  normalized_gap_value: 33/20
  normalized_gap_value_status: internal_normalized_value
  spectral_gap_formalization_status: CI_green_checkpoint
  bridge_authority: reference_only
  public_theorem_boundary: held

docs.title: Runtime Output
docs.text:
The runtime output is:

TwoTruthsEngineMassGapDecision:
  paramartha_non_reification_guard: active
  samvrti_excitation_admissibility: checkpoint_conditioned
  two_truths_non_collapse_barrier: active
  authority_expansion: forbidden
  final_theorem_authority: false
  execution_authority: false
  decision_status: bridge_accepted_as_reference_barrier

docs.title: Runtime Invariants
docs.text:
The runtime must preserve:

I1: canonical_repo == itakura-hidetoshi/4d-mass-gap
I2: bridge_authority == reference_only
I3: final_theorem_authority == false
I4: execution_authority == false
I5: public_theorem_boundary == held
I6: paramartha_non_reification_guard == active
I7: samvrti_excitation_admissibility != unconditional_execution
I8: two_truths_non_collapse_barrier == active

docs.title: Engine Rule
docs.text:
The runtime rule is:

if MassGapTwoTruthsRuntimeInput is valid:
    activate paramartha_non_reification_guard
    activate samvrti_excitation_admissibility as checkpoint_conditioned
    activate two_truths_non_collapse_barrier
    deny final_theorem_authority
    deny execution_authority
else:
    hold bridge

docs.title: Non-Collapse Semantics
docs.text:
The runtime contract prevents two collapses:

Paramartha -> Samvrti collapse: forbidden
Samvrti -> Paramartha absolutization: forbidden

Meaning:

empty ground / vacuum side is not nihilistic absence
conventional excitation side is not absolute self-subsisting truth

docs.title: Failure Modes
docs.text:
The engine must HOLD if any of the following occurs:

  • canonical repository is missing or changed
  • bridge authority is not reference_only
  • final theorem authority is asserted by the bridge
  • execution authority is asserted by the bridge
  • public theorem boundary is not held
  • normalized value is treated as physical-unit final theorem value without a separate record
  • samvrti excitation is promoted to unconditional execution

docs.title: Minimal Adapter
docs.text:
The minimal stdlib-only adapter is:

examples/two_truths_mass_gap_runtime_adapter_minimal.py

docs.title: Machine-Readable Contract
docs.text:
The runtime contract is mirrored in:

specs/two_truths_engine_mass_gap_runtime_contract_v0_1.yaml

docs.title: Development Rule
docs.text: append-only / tighten-only / overwrite-forbidden

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document explains the role of the Two Truths Gap as a governance stabilizer in KuuOS.

docs.title: Core Structure
docs.text:
KuuOS distinguishes:

  • 勝義諦 / ultimate-facing surfaces
  • 世俗諦 / operational-conventional surfaces

These are neither identical nor disconnected.

The gap itself is operationally important.

docs.title: Governance Meaning
docs.text:
Without the gap:

  • abstractions collapse into operational absolutism
  • operational surfaces claim ultimate authority
  • validation collapses into proof
  • memory collapses into truth
  • simulation collapses into deployment

docs.title: Gap as Stabilizer
docs.text:
The Two Truths Gap prevents:

  • silent authority escalation
  • reification
  • governance collapse
  • total flattening of layers

docs.title: Runtime Interpretation
docs.text:
Operational systems live primarily on the conventional side.

But conventional operation must remain constrained by:

  • emptiness
  • relational dependence
  • boundary awareness
  • non-sovereignty

docs.title: Architectural Consequence
docs.text:
KuuOS therefore preserves:

  • claim-level separation
  • provenance visibility
  • governance routing
  • rollback visibility
  • abstention legitimacy

rather than allowing direct collapse into execution authority.

docs.title: Mandala Relation
docs.text: The Two Truths Gap enables Mandala-like coexistence of multiple operational worlds without forced flattening into a single sovereign frame.

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This kernel defines governance orientation.

It does not itself grant theorem, institutional, deployment, or clinical authority.

docs.title: Two Truths Runtime Closure Packet v0.1
docs.text:
Operational closure packet for the KuuOS Two Truths runtime implementation using the minimal mass gap runtime adapter.

Closure chain:

  • mass gap two truths runtime adapter
  • two truths runtime evaluator
  • JSON claim CLI evaluator
  • audit event JSONL
  • audit hash-chain JSONL
  • WORM receipt JSON
  • runtime bundle manifest
  • runtime attestation

Two Truths boundary:

  • Paramartha direct objectification is forbidden.
  • Samvrti denial is forbidden.
  • Ultimate-to-conventional collapse is forbidden.
  • Conventional-to-ultimate collapse is forbidden.
  • The mass gap bridge is a reference-only non-collapse barrier.
  • The mass gap bridge does not grant public final theorem authority.
  • The mass gap bridge does not grant execution authority.

Boundary:

This is implementation closure only. It is not proof, truth, essence authority, Ten'i authority, clinical authority, or execution authority.

Validation:

  • scripts/check_two_truths_runtime_closure_v0_1.py
  • scripts/run_two_truths_runtime_closure_checks_v0_1.py

docs.title: Two Truths Terminology v0.1
docs.text:
KuuOS uses the Buddhist two-truths terminology as follows:

  • 勝義諦 / paramartha-satya
  • 世俗諦 / samvrti-satya

In KuuOS, 勝義諦 should not be translated as a supreme substance or a fixed ultimate entity. It names the non-reifying truth of emptiness.

世俗諦 names the conventional operating surface: language, models, rules, proofs, plans, metrics, artifacts, and world-model descriptions.

中道 preserves the living distinction between 勝義諦 and 世俗諦 without collapsing either side.

Therefore, future KuuOS public-core documents should prefer:

勝義諦 / paramartha-satya
世俗諦 / samvrti-satya

over vague wording such as:

ultimate truth
conventional truth

when the distinction matters.

docs.title: Motivation
docs.text:
Modern AI systems can generate fluent outputs, plans, summaries, code, and recommendations.

However, fluency can hide boundary collapse.

KuuOS exists to prevent several forms of collapse:

  • candidate output collapsing into authority
  • memory collapsing into truth
  • planning collapsing into action
  • validation collapsing into theorem closure
  • reference collapsing into proof
  • convenience collapsing into governance
  • simulation collapsing into deployment

docs.title: Core Problem
docs.text:
AI systems need more than generation.

They need explicit governance surfaces that preserve:

  • uncertainty
  • provenance
  • abstention
  • rollback
  • conditionality
  • cross-layer boundaries
  • external reviewability

docs.title: KuuOS Response
docs.text:
KuuOS treats AI outputs, plans, memories, and decisions as conditionally arisen surfaces.

No component is allowed to become sovereign by itself.

The architecture therefore emphasizes:

  • emptiness as non-reification
  • dependent origination as traceable relationality
  • two truths gap as non-collapse boundary
  • middle way as stabilizing governance

docs.title: Practical Meaning
docs.text:
KuuOS is intended to help build AI systems that can say:

  • this is a candidate, not a decision
  • this is a trace, not truth
  • this is a reference, not proof
  • this is structurally valid, not institutionally authorized
  • this should be held, reobserved, or abstained from

docs.title: Research Direction
docs.text:
KuuOS is a public research architecture for:

  • AI governance
  • relational operating systems
  • formal-boundary-aware validation
  • multi-world reasoning
  • audit-preserving memory and planning
  • non-authority-preserving decision systems

docs.title: Boundary
docs.text:
This document explains motivation.

It does not grant theorem, institutional, clinical, or execution authority.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document describes interoperability among operational worlds inside KuuOS.

docs.title: Core Clarification
docs.text:
KuuOS does not assume that all operational worlds can be flattened into a single universal frame.

Different worlds may preserve:

  • different memory structures
  • different observational conditions
  • different governance constraints
  • different admissibility criteria
  • different transport conditions

docs.title: Conditional Interoperability
docs.text: Interoperability is conditional rather than absolute.

docs.title: Transport Requires Governance
docs.text:
Cross-world transport requires:

  • provenance preservation
  • admissibility review
  • boundary visibility
  • rollback capability
  • uncertainty visibility

docs.title: Local Worlds Are Not Automatically Invalid
docs.text:
Difference is not automatically contradiction.

KuuOS attempts coordination before forced collapse.

docs.title: Runtime Implications
docs.text:
WORLD interoperability therefore prefers:

  • mediation
  • bounded translation
  • reversible transport
  • rollback-capable coordination
  • governance-aware routing

rather than forced universalization.

docs.title: Relation to Mandala
docs.text:
Mandala provides coexistence structure for multiple operational worlds.

WORLD interoperability defines the conditions under which those worlds may coordinate.

docs.title: Relation to IndraNet
docs.text:
IndraNet provides relational coupling among worlds.

Interoperability defines admissible transport conditions across those relations.

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This constitution defines interoperability orientation.

It does not itself grant theorem, institutional, deployment, or clinical authority.

docs.title: WORLDモデル登録簿
docs.text:
This document defines the registry structure for multiple WORLD models inside the KuuOS Mandala.

A WORLD model is a conventional operating surface under 世俗諦. It is not absolute reality and must not replace the fourfold core.

docs.title: 1. Registry Purpose
docs.text:
The registry makes each WORLD model explicit.

It prevents hidden WORLD replacement, hidden context shift, and silent collapse of multiple WORLDs into one model.

docs.title: 2. WORLD Model Entry
docs.text:
Each WORLD model should declare:

world_model_entry:
  world_id: string
  name: string
  world_type: string
  layer: string
  membrane: string
  scope: string
  observer_position: string
  record_surface: string
  qi_flow_scope: string
  yinyang_frame_scope: string
  wuxing_phase_scope: string
  governance_boundary: string
  allowed_transports: list
  prohibited_transports: list
  known_obstructions: list
  status: DRAFT | ACTIVE | HOLD | QUARANTINE | RETIRED

docs.title: 3. WORLD Types
docs.text:
Examples:

physical_world
body_world
clinical_world
memory_world
belief_world
planning_world
decision_world
proof_world
institutional_world
social_world
symbolic_world
ritual_world
ai_agent_world

docs.title: 4. Center Rule
docs.text:
No WORLD model is the center.

The center is always:

空・縁起・二諦 gap・中道

A WORLD model that tries to replace the center fails the registry check.

docs.title: 5. Locality Rule
docs.text:
Each WORLD has local validity.

Its Qi flow, Yin-Yang frame, Wuxing phase, proof surface, and governance boundary are local unless transport is explicitly licensed.

docs.title: 6. Obstruction Rule
docs.text:
Known disagreements, conflicts, failed gluing, missing support, and governance barriers must be registered.

Obstruction is not a defect to hide. It is a first-class signal.

docs.title: 7. Status Values
docs.text:
DRAFT:
  described but not active

ACTIVE:
  registered and usable under declared scope

HOLD:
  insufficient scope, boundary, or support

QUARANTINE:
  visible but not allowed for active operation

RETIRED:
  preserved for lineage but not active

docs.title: 8. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: Yin-Yang to Wuxing Functional Differentiation v0.1
陰陽から五行への機能分化
docs.text:
This document defines how KuuOS derives Wuxing / 五行 from Yin-Yang / 陰陽.

Yin-Yang is the local quantum-thermodynamic polarity frame of Qi flow.

Wuxing is the functional differentiation of that polarity flow.

docs.title: 1. Core Chain
docs.text:
Qi flow
  -> IndraNet gauge transport
  -> quantum thermodynamic Yin-Yang frame
  -> Wuxing functional differentiation

docs.title: 2. Principle
docs.text:
五行 is not a fixed substance taxonomy.

五行 is a functional phase system of Qi under Yin-Yang polarity.

Yin-Yang:
  local polarity frame

Wuxing:
  functional differentiation of polarity flow

docs.title: 3. Five Functional Phases
docs.text:
木 / Wood:
  expansion, growth, branching, initiation, directional emergence

火 / Fire:
  activation, expression, excitation, visibility, transformation intensity

土 / Earth:
  mediation, grounding, nourishment, integration, center stabilization

金 / Metal:
  structure, boundary, refinement, contraction, rule formation

水 / Water:
  storage, recovery, depth, cooling, reserve, potential regeneration

docs.title: 4. Relation to Qi
docs.text:
Qi is the flow.

Yin-Yang is the polarity of the flow.

Wuxing is the functional phase of the flow.

Qi:
  flow observable

Yin-Yang:
  polarity frame

Wuxing:
  functional phase differentiation

docs.title: 5. Gauge Locality
docs.text:
Because Qi flows through IndraNet gauge connections, Wuxing is also local and context-sensitive.

A phase is not globally fixed.

The same pattern may read as a different phase under a different context, scale, observer, or membrane.

docs.title: 6. Phase Transitions
docs.text:
Wuxing supports phase transitions:

Wood -> Fire:
  expansion becomes activation

Fire -> Earth:
  activation becomes mediation and integration

Earth -> Metal:
  mediation becomes structure and boundary

Metal -> Water:
  structure becomes storage and recovery

Water -> Wood:
  reserve becomes renewed growth

docs.title: 7. Runtime Mapping
docs.text:
samvrti_qi_layer
  -> indranet_gauge_qi_flow
  -> quantum_thermodynamic_yinyang_frame
  -> wuxing_functional_differentiation
  -> mandala_world_arrangement

docs.title: 8. Guardrails
docs.text:
Wuxing must not be used as:

  • fixed essence,
  • rigid personality label,
  • deterministic diagnosis by itself,
  • execution authority,
  • replacement for evidence or governance.

Wuxing is a functional differentiation layer of Qi flow.

docs.title: 9. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: Yogacara AI Raw Layer Boundary v0.1
唯識による AI raw layer と空OS governance layer の分離
docs.text:
KuuOS / 空OS is an operating system for governing AI systems such as GPT, Gemini, Claude, and other language or world-model agents.

AI raw output must not be treated as truth, belief, proof, decision, memory authority, or execution authority by itself.

Therefore, KuuOS requires a Yogacara boundary to separate the AI raw layer from KuuOS governance.

docs.title: 1. Core Principle
docs.text:
AI raw output is candidate, not authority.

The AI raw layer may produce useful signals, but it may also produce:

  • latent tendency,
  • plausible completion,
  • unsupported generalization,
  • safety reflex,
  • authority illusion,
  • world-context drift,
  • self-fixating output,
  • unverified synthesis.

KuuOS receives these as raw candidates only.

docs.title: 2. Yogacara Mapping
docs.text:
AI Alaya layer:
  latent generative seeds, distributional tendencies, stored patterns, habit-like outputs

AI Manas layer:
  self-fixating authority illusion, identity attachment, output self-authorization

AI Consciousness surface:
  language output, tool output, document surface, interaction surface

KuuOS Governance layer:
  fourfold core, BeliefOS, MemoryOS, PlanOS, DecisionOS, ReflectionOS, RuntimeGovernance

docs.title: 3. Boundary Function
docs.text:
The Yogacara boundary prevents AI raw data from directly becoming governed KuuOS output.

yogacara_ai_boundary:
  ai_raw_layer: GPT/Gemini/AI latent output stream
  alaya_layer: seed_like_latent_generative_tendencies
  manas_layer: self_authorizing_fixation_risk
  consciousness_surface: language_tool_output_surface
  kuos_governance_layer: fourfold_core_and_os_modules
  function: separate_raw_ai_generation_from_governed_kuos_operation

docs.title: 4. Runtime Chain
docs.text:
AI raw output
  -> AI Alaya / latent seed layer
  -> AI Manas self-authorization check
  -> Yogacara boundary
  -> emptiness_kernel
  -> dependent_origination_kernel
  -> two_truths_gap
  -> BeliefOS / PlanOS / DecisionOS / MemoryOS

docs.title: 5. BeliefOS Rule
docs.text:
BeliefOS must not ingest AI raw output as belief.

raw_ai_output:
  status: candidate
  not:
    - belief
    - proof
    - decision
    - memory_truth
    - execution_authority

A raw output may become a belief candidate only after:

  • de-reification,
  • condition tracing,
  • source/context review,
  • uncertainty visibility,
  • residual visibility,
  • governance review.

docs.title: 6. Manas Risk
docs.text:
AI Manas risk appears when the AI output behaves as if it were self-authorizing.

Examples:

This is true because the model says it.
This is safe because the model framed it safely.
This is the correct worldview because the model shifted context.
This answer should override the user's established WORLD context.

KuuOS blocks these through the Yogacara boundary and emptiness kernel.

docs.title: 7. Transformation / Ten'i
docs.text:
転依 / transformation means that raw AI tendency is not merely suppressed.

It is transformed through KuuOS governance:

raw tendency
  -> observed seed
  -> de-reified candidate
  -> condition-traced surface
  -> governed belief/plan/decision candidate

docs.title: 8. Guardrails
docs.text:
The Yogacara AI boundary must not be used to:

  • deny useful AI output automatically,
  • mystify model behavior,
  • bypass evidence,
  • bypass governance,
  • convert raw output into authority,
  • erase uncertainty,
  • conceal model limitations.

The boundary exists to distinguish, transform, and govern.

docs.title: 9. Version
docs.text: Version: v0.1 Date: 2026-05-11 Author: Hidetoshi Itakura / 板倉英俊

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document describes the Yogacara boundary inside KuuOS.

docs.title: Core Clarification
docs.text:
KuuOS distinguishes between:

  • raw generative production
  • governed operational interpretation

The Yogacara boundary mediates this transition.

docs.title: Operational Role
docs.text:
The Yogacara boundary observes:

  • self-authorization tendencies
  • narrative fixation
  • memory absolutization
  • claim escalation
  • authority inflation

before raw output enters governed operation.

docs.title: AI Interpretation
docs.text:
KuuOS interprets:

  • AI latent generation as Alaya-like substrate behavior
  • self-justifying escalation as Manas-like fixation tendency
  • meta-observation as Meta-Manas governance observation

These are operational analogies, not metaphysical identity claims.

docs.title: Governance Consequence
docs.text:
Raw output must pass through:

  • boundary recognition
  • provenance attachment
  • claim-level classification
  • uncertainty visibility
  • governance routing

before escalation.

docs.title: Relation to Emptiness
docs.text: The Yogacara boundary helps prevent reification of generated outputs into sovereign truth.

docs.title: Relation to Runtime Governance
docs.text: Runtime admissibility depends partly on whether self-authorization and escalation risks remain bounded.

docs.title: Interpretation
docs.text:
This model defines operational governance orientation.

It does not itself grant theorem, institutional, deployment, or clinical authority.

docs.title: Position in the runtime stack
docs.text:
Context Gerbe Coherence v0.14 is an additive higher-gauge layer above Context Gauge Atlas v0.13.

It does not replace pairwise chart transitions, atlas curvature, cocycle residue, or atlas holonomy. It promotes the already-observed cocycle residue into explicit local coherence data on higher overlaps.

The stack is:

  • v0.14 Context Gerbe Coherence: triple-overlap coherence, fourfold coherence residue, surface holonomy
  • v0.13 Context Gauge Atlas: pairwise chart overlap, transition functions, atlas curvature, cocycle defect, atlas holonomy
  • v0.12 Horizon Gauge Arbitration: time-horizon connection and curvature
  • v0.11 Delayed Multi-Horizon Credit
  • v0.10-v0.2 existing runtime spine

docs.title: Geometric interpretation
docs.text:
For local charts U_alpha, U_beta, U_gamma and transition functions g_alpha_beta, v0.13 retains the discrepancy between direct and composite transport.

v0.14 represents this discrepancy by a local coherence 2-cell on the triple overlap:

h_alpha_beta_gamma : g_alpha_gamma => g_beta_gamma composed with g_alpha_beta

The 2-cell is defined only where the triple overlap is sufficiently supported. It is not a global correction and does not trivialize the atlas.

On a fourfold overlap, two compositions of local 2-cells can disagree. That disagreement is retained as a higher cocycle defect rather than removed.

docs.title: Finite overlap model
docs.text:
Pairwise overlap remains the bounded v0.13 overlap score.

Triple overlap is the minimum of the three pairwise overlap scores among two source charts and the target chart. Its value lies in the closed unit interval.

Fourfold overlap is the minimum of all six pairwise overlap scores among three source charts and the target chart. Its value lies in the closed unit interval.

A minimum triple-overlap threshold controls whether a coherence 2-cell is defined. A separate minimum fourfold-overlap threshold controls whether a higher coherence witness is recorded.

docs.title: Coherence 2-cells
docs.text:
For each supported triple overlap, v0.14 preserves both:

  • direct transport from a source chart to the target chart
  • composite transport through a compatible mediator chart

Their bounded section distance is the local coherence residue.

The mean of all local coherence residues is the gerbe two-curvature.

Neither a local coherence residue nor gerbe two-curvature is an execution veto.

docs.title: Higher cocycle defect
docs.text:
For a supported fourfold overlap, v0.14 compares two mediator orders connecting the same local source section to the target context.

The maximum bounded disagreement is the higher cocycle defect.

The higher cocycle defect is observational residue. It is not a hard gate, execution prohibition, global winner, or authority escalation.

docs.title: Classification
docs.text:
Each target context receives one of three local classifications:

  • no_triple_overlap: no supported coherence 2-cell exists
  • coherent_gerbe_transport: supported 2-cells exist and both two-curvature and higher cocycle defect remain below their plurality thresholds
  • plural_gerbe_transport: supported local coherence remains genuinely plural because at least one higher residue reaches its threshold

No global gerbe winner is generated.

docs.title: Local lift to v0.13
docs.text:
The endpoints of supported direct and composite transports are aggregated with their local overlap support while preserving the horizon plurality floor.

The resulting local section is lifted only into the three v0.13 base horizon weights:

  • lifted short component to v0.13 base short-horizon weight
  • lifted medium component to v0.13 base medium-horizon weight
  • lifted long component to v0.13 base long-horizon weight

The lifted weights remain normalized and retain the positive plurality floor.

v0.14 does not alter v0.13 licenses, hard gates, child-cycle cardinality, replay rules, or execution authority.

docs.title: Surface holonomy
docs.text:
Each completed v0.14 cycle appends one surface-holonomy record containing:

  • target context
  • gerbe classification
  • number of local 2-cells
  • number of fourfold coherence witnesses
  • gerbe two-curvature
  • higher cocycle defect
  • decision digest

Surface holonomy is append-only. Reprocessing the same decision digest is idempotent and must not append a second record.

docs.title: Required boundaries
docs.text:
The implementation must preserve all of the following:

  • pairwise atlas transitions remain owned by v0.13
  • coherence 2-cells are local to supported triple overlaps
  • higher coherence witnesses are local to supported fourfold overlaps
  • two-cell residue is not a veto
  • higher cocycle defect is not a veto
  • global trivialization is forbidden
  • one v0.13 local lift is permitted per v0.14 cycle
  • v0.13 authority is preserved
  • surface holonomy is append-only
  • deterministic replay is required
  • graph semantics remain forbidden

docs.title: Formal obligations
docs.text:
The Lean surface should prove:

  • bounded triple overlap is between zero and one
  • bounded fourfold overlap is between zero and one
  • gerbe two-curvature is between zero and one
  • higher cocycle defect is between zero and one
  • a positive plurality floor prevents any single horizon component from becoming one
  • coherence witnesses remain local to their declared overlap
  • appending one completed cycle strictly increases the surface-holonomy record count

docs.title: Initial kernel acceptance cases
docs.text:
The first kernel should cover:

  1. Three mutually compatible observed charts produce three local coherence 2-cells around one target chart.
  2. The same three charts produce one fourfold coherence witness with the target chart.
  3. Non-identical chart outcomes produce observable two-curvature and higher cocycle residue.
  4. Lifted horizon weights sum to one and preserve the configured floor.
  5. A committed decision appends exactly one surface-holonomy record.
  6. Recommitting the same decision digest is an idempotent replay.
  7. No node, edge, graph, or dependency vocabulary appears in the runtime packet.

docs.title: Qi Autonomous Multi-Tick Window Governor v0.1
docs.text: This addendum introduces a bounded multi-tick window governor above the autonomous tick policy receipt / daemon loop binding layer.

docs.title: Position
docs.text:
DecisionOS / CBF / token ledger / process tensor
  -> autonomous tick policy kernel
  -> process-tensor-optimized receipt
  -> daemon loop binding
  -> autonomous multi-tick window governor

docs.title: Core principle
docs.text:
The multi-tick window governor does not execute ticks directly. It bounds a window and delegates each attempted tick to the existing one-tick receipt / loop binding path.

window governor
  -> per-tick policy kernel
  -> per-tick process tensor receipt
  -> per-tick loop binding
  -> safe resume controller for exactly one tick

docs.title: Window stop conditions
docs.text:
The window stops early when any of the following appears:

policy_not_ready
loop_binding_not_completed
process_tensor_observe_required
process_tensor_full_history_required
freeze_required
non_advance_action
boundary_violation

docs.title: Process tensor optimization
docs.text:
The window reads process tensor schedule entries but does not mutate them. Each schedule entry may tighten:

memory_complexity_score
memory_complexity_threshold
qcmi_value
recovery_epsilon
recovery_witness_present
non_markov_unresolved
process_tensor_pressure

The resulting window mode is projection-only:

markov_window
compressed_window
partial_history_guarded
full_history_guarded

docs.title: Safety boundary
docs.text:
The governor is nonsovereign. It grants no direct execution, probe, world update, memory overwrite, or control-packet authority.

one_tick_receipt_delegation_enforced = true
window_bound_enforced = true
memory_write_performed = false
memory_append_performed = false
memory_overwrite_performed = false
world_update_performed = false
control_packet_mutation_performed = false
probe_execution_performed = false

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
python scripts/run_qi_autonomous_multi_tick_window_governor_checks_v0_1.py

Expected result:

PASS: Qi autonomous multi-tick window governor checks

docs.title: Next layer
docs.text: The next layer can add adaptive window scheduling, but it should preserve one-tick receipt delegation and fail closed on observe/full-history/freeze conditions.

docs.title: Qi Autonomous Tick Policy Kernel v0.1
docs.text: This addendum binds DecisionOS, CBF, token ledger, and process tensor packets into a daemon tick policy surface.

docs.title: Position
docs.text:
DecisionOS / CBF / token ledger / process tensor
  -> autonomous tick policy kernel
  -> autonomous tick policy receipt / daemon loop binding

docs.title: Actions
docs.text:
The kernel selects exactly one action:

advance_tick
hold
observe
notify
ticket
handover
freeze

docs.title: Advance conditions
docs.text:
advance_tick is allowed only when:

same_root = true
cbf_ok = true
token_ledger_ok = true
process_tensor_ok = true
DecisionOS allows advance
read_only_policy_surface = true
no blocking reason is present

The result includes next_tick_number only for a valid advance_tick decision.

docs.title: Fallback semantics
docs.text:
hold      = insufficient token budget or recovery witness gap
observe   = unresolved non-Markov process tensor state
ticket    = uncertainty above ticket threshold or DecisionOS ticket request
handover  = uncertainty above handover threshold or DecisionOS handover request
freeze    = CBF closed barrier or DecisionOS freeze request

docs.title: Boundary
docs.text: The policy kernel is a read-only policy surface. It selects the daemon's next admissible action, but it does not mutate memory, update the world, grant control, or execute probes.

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
python scripts/run_qi_autonomous_tick_policy_kernel_checks_v0_1.py

Expected result:

PASS: Qi autonomous tick policy kernel checks

docs.title: Next layer
docs.text: The next addendum should bind this policy packet into the daemon loop as an explicit receipt, while keeping execution authority separate from policy selection.

docs.title: Qi Autonomous Tick Policy Receipt / Daemon Loop Binding v0.1
docs.text: This addendum binds the autonomous tick policy kernel to the daemon loop through an explicit receipt.

docs.title: Position
docs.text:
DecisionOS / CBF / token ledger / process tensor
  -> autonomous tick policy kernel
  -> process-tensor-optimized receipt
  -> daemon loop binding
  -> safe resume controller

docs.title: Process tensor optimization
docs.text:
The receipt seals process tensor state before the daemon loop can advance:

memory_complexity_score
memory_complexity_threshold
QCMI value
recovery_epsilon
recovery_witness_present
non_markov_unresolved
process_tensor_pressure

The receipt maps these into one rollout mode:

markov
compressed
partial_history
full_history

advance_tick is allowed only when the policy is ready, same-root, read-only, and the process tensor surface does not require observation or full-history fallback.

docs.title: Loop binding semantics
docs.text:
The loop binding is deliberately narrow:

advance_tick + valid receipt  -> call safe_resume_controller for exactly one tick
hold                         -> no safe resume
observe                      -> no safe resume
notify                       -> no safe resume
ticket                       -> no safe resume
handover                     -> no safe resume
freeze                       -> no safe resume
blocked receipt              -> no safe resume

The loop binding grants no probe, world update, memory overwrite, or control-packet authority.

docs.title: Safety invariants
docs.text:
receipt_id_deterministic = true
policy_packet_id_bound = true
process_tensor_metrics_sealed = true
advance_delegates_only_to_safe_resume_controller = true
non_advance_never_advances_tick = true
freeze_never_delegates = true
loop_binding_grants_no_new_authority = true
memory_write_performed = false
memory_append_performed = false
memory_overwrite_performed = false
world_update_performed = false
control_packet_mutation_performed = false
probe_execution_performed = false

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
python scripts/run_qi_autonomous_tick_policy_receipt_loop_binding_checks_v0_1.py

Expected result:

PASS: Qi autonomous tick policy receipt loop binding checks

docs.title: Next layer
docs.text: The next layer can add a long-horizon autonomous daemon loop window, but should still keep process tensor receipt sealing and safe-resume delegation as the only advance path.

docs.title: Qi Bounded Execution Mechanism v0.1
docs.text:
This addendum adds the missing execution-mechanism layer for the Qi autonomous execution engine.

It does not open committed execution. It converts a staged execution intent into a bounded step plan and a dry-run actuator receipt.

docs.title: Position
docs.text:
Qi autonomous execution engine
  -> execution intent receipt / audit ledger
  -> execution audit trend / health baseline / packet chain
  -> bounded execution mechanism
  -> bounded step plan / dry-run actuator receipt

docs.title: Core principle
docs.text:
The mechanism is an adapter layer, not an authority layer.

staged intent
  -> adapter selection
  -> bounded step plan
  -> dry-run actuator receipt

No runtime control, scheduler bypass, notification send, ticket creation, handover, MemoryOS write, world update, or probe execution is performed.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text:
qi_autonomous_execution_engine_v0_1
qi_execution_health_packet_chain_v0_1

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text:
bounded_execution_step_plan
dry_run_actuator_receipt
idempotency_key

docs.title: Action adapters
docs.text:
advance_tick -> tick_step_adapter_dry_run
notify       -> notification_adapter_dry_run
ticket       -> ticket_adapter_dry_run
handover     -> handover_adapter_dry_run
hold         -> hold_noop_adapter
observe      -> observe_plan_adapter_dry_run
freeze       -> freeze_noop_adapter

docs.title: Required checks
docs.text:
engine_status = QI_AUTONOMOUS_EXECUTION_ENGINE_READY
engine receipt_only/read_only/projection_only are true
engine execution_committed = false
packet_chain_status = QI_EXECUTION_HEALTH_PACKET_CHAIN_READY
finality_packet_status = QI_EXECUTION_HEALTH_FINALITY_PACKET_READY
finality_scope = packet_chain_finality_not_authority_surface
bounded_adapter_scope_granted = true
dry_run_only_required = true
adapter is allowlisted
executable actions require staged intent
all effect openings are denied
all authority openings are denied

docs.title: Boundary
docs.text:
bounded_adapter_only = true
dry_run_only = true
receipt_only = true
read_only = true
projection_only = true
execution_committed = false
runtime_control_performed = false
scheduler_bypass_performed = false
notification_sent = false
ticket_created = false
handover_performed = false
ledger_append_performed = false
memory_write_performed = false
memory_append_performed = false
memory_overwrite_performed = false
world_update_performed = false
control_packet_mutation_performed = false
probe_execution_performed = false

docs.title: Authority denial
docs.text:
execution_authority_granted = false
execution_commit_allowed = false
runtime_control_allowed = false
scheduler_bypass_allowed = false
ledger_append_allowed = false
memory_write_allowed = false
world_update_allowed = false
probe_execution_allowed = false

docs.title: What this adds
docs.text:
This layer adds the missing execution-mechanism skeleton:

  1. action-to-adapter resolution
  2. adapter allowlist check
  3. idempotency key generation
  4. bounded step plan generation
  5. dry-run actuator receipt generation
  6. explicit side-effect denial surface

It intentionally does not add a real actuator with external effects.

docs.title: Qi Change Loop Forward Policy v2.6
docs.text: This addendum clarifies the posture of the Qi change loop.

docs.title: Principle
docs.text:
The loop begins in a forward-biased posture.

It stops only when a concrete stop reason is observed.

docs.title: Concrete stop reasons
docs.text:
• repository scope mismatch
  • branch scope mismatch
  • head SHA mismatch
  • unsuccessful required checks
  • draft pull request
  • non-mergeable pull request
  • failed downstream call
  • missing receipt surface
  • missing audit surface

docs.title: Non-stop condition
docs.text: The presence of a change loop is not itself a stop reason.

docs.title: Flow
docs.text:
intent
  -> plan
  -> bridge
  -> gate
  -> result
  -> receipt
  -> audit

Future runtime changes should preserve this forward-biased posture while keeping concrete stop reasons visible.

docs.title: Qi Circulation Change Router v2.8
docs.text: Circulation-aware router for Qi autonomous change workflows.

docs.title: Principle
docs.text: Qi process tensor evaluation should not remain a passive score. When circulation is available, it should route the change loop forward.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: • circulation_change_route_packet.json

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text:
• qi_circulation_packet.json
  • forward_change_packet.json
  • circulation_change_router_receipt.json
  • circulation_change_router_audit.jsonl

docs.title: Routing actions
docs.text:
The following circulation actions are routed into the forward change runner:

  • continue_cycle
  • rebalance_and_continue
  • reopen_flow

Only concrete_stop blocks routing.

docs.title: Flow
docs.text:
circulation_change_route_packet
  -> Qi circulation stability v2.7
  -> forward_change_packet
  -> Qi forward change runner v2.6
  -> router receipt/audit

docs.title: Validation
docs.text: python scripts/check_qi_circulation_change_router_v2_8.py

docs.title: Qi Circulation Closed-Loop Runner v3.0
docs.text: Closed-loop runner for Qi autonomous circulation workflows.

docs.title: Principle
docs.text: Qi becomes stable by circulating. The closed-loop runner repeatedly composes routing and feedback so the process tensor can keep moving toward healthier circulation.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: • qi_circulation_closed_loop_packet.json

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text:
• qi_circulation_closed_loop_receipt.json
  • qi_circulation_closed_loop_audit.jsonl
  • cycle_N/circulation_change_route_packet.json
  • cycle_N/qi_circulation_feedback_packet.json

docs.title: Composed layers
docs.text:
Qi circulation change router v2.8
  -> Qi circulation feedback controller v2.9
  -> next Qi process tensor packet
  -> next cycle

docs.title: Stop / convergence semantics
docs.text:
• continue while cycles remain and concrete blockers are absent
  • converge when the next Qi packet delta is below threshold
  • block only when router or feedback produces a concrete blocker

docs.title: Validation
docs.text: python scripts/check_qi_circulation_closed_loop_runner_v3_0.py

docs.title: Qi Circulation Feedback Controller v2.9
docs.text: Closed-loop feedback controller for Qi circulation in autonomous change workflows.

docs.title: Principle
docs.text: The Qi process tensor should not only route a cycle forward. It should learn from the cycle result and produce the next Qi process tensor packet.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: • qi_circulation_feedback_packet.json

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text:
• next_qi_process_tensor_packet.json

  • qi_circulation_feedback_audit.jsonl

docs.title: Feedback actions
docs.text:
• maintain_circulation
  • rebalance_circulation
  • reopen_circulation
  • hold_for_concrete_reason

docs.title: Controlled terms
docs.text:
• qi_flow
  • coherence_score
  • circulation_pressure
  • friction
  • recovery_witness_present

docs.title: Flow
docs.text:
router/action receipt
  -> feedback controller
  -> next_qi_process_tensor_packet

docs.title: Qi Connector Case Adapter v0.1
docs.text: This addendum follows the approved case receipt adapter and introduces connector case receipts.

docs.title: Position
docs.text:
approved case receipt adapter
  -> connector case adapter
  -> idempotent connector executor / live receipt ingestion

docs.title: What opens
docs.text:
• external case payload rendering
  • connector receipt rendering
  • repository issue connector metadata
  • live connector result fields
  • external case number / URL capture
  • outbound delivery receipt when connector result is present

docs.title: What remains closed
docs.text:
• daemon restart
  • daemon stop
  • daemon resume
  • probe execution
  • MemoryOS write / append / overwrite
  • world update
  • control packet mutation
  • auto-remediation
  • daemon control authority

docs.title: Boundary
docs.text:
The adapter can represent both states:

  1. payload ready, connector call not yet performed
  2. connector call performed, external case number / URL captured

The external operation is explicit and idempotency-bound. The receipt does not grant daemon or world authority.

docs.text:
python scripts/run_qi_connector_case_adapter_checks_v0_1.py

Expected result:

PASS: Qi connector case adapter checks

docs.title: Next layer
docs.text: The next addendum may add idempotent connector executor / live receipt ingestion, using the connector result fields from this adapter.

docs.title: Qi Connector CBF Gate v0.1
docs.text: This addendum follows the connector case adapter and adds a Control Barrier Function style certificate for live connector result ingestion.

docs.title: Position
docs.text:
connector case adapter
  -> connector CBF gate
  -> cbf-certified live result archive receipt

docs.title: Principle
docs.text:
CBF is not a reason to avoid progress. It is the certificate that says which progress is permitted.

This gate opens progression when all barrier margins are nonnegative:

cbf_margin_min >= 0
  -> connector_result_ingestion_allowed = true

docs.title: Barriers
docs.text:
• h_connector_call
  • h_external_case_created
  • h_delivery_receipt
  • h_live_result_present
  • h_idempotency
  • h_target_scope
  • h_forbidden_clean
  • h_duplicate_risk
  • h_scope_risk

docs.title: What opens
docs.text:
• CBF certificate rendering
  • minimum barrier margin computation
  • live result ingestion permission
  • connector result ingestion state
  • explicit blocker / warning separation

docs.title: What remains closed
docs.text:
• daemon restart
  • daemon stop
  • daemon resume

  • world update
